title: Pentanema oculus-christi
taxonomy: Pentanema oculus-christi is classified within the family Asteraceae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants in the same family. Pentanema oculus-christi is further classified within the genus Pentanema, which is a group of flowering plants. The species is also sometimes classified as a synonym of other species within the same genus, but its primary classification is within the Asteraceae family. Further research may be necessary to confirm the exact classification of Pentanema oculus-christi.
uses: Pentanema oculus-christi, a plant species found in the Mediterranean region, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for various purposes. The plant's leaves and stems are rich in bioactive compounds, which may contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditional uses of Pentanema oculus-christi include treatment of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and indigestion, as well as relief from respiratory problems like coughs and colds. Furthermore, the plant has been reported to have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in wound healing and reducing inflammation. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal potential and effects on human health.

intro: Pentanema oculus-christi, also known as Christ's eye, is a perennial plant species in the family Asteraceae, characterized by its distinctive white or pale yellow daisy-like flowers with a dark brown or purple center, typically found in dry and disturbed areas. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Pentanema oculus-christi include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, such as pentanemine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Pentanema oculus-christi is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects.
